acceptance_targets: [CH05_PROSE_POV_ABSTRACTION] In the revised snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:25-35 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:51-55, a cold reader can still identify that sight, hearing, and proprioception vanish and that Kai dislocates a shoulder and breaks a wrist, but the passage does not depend on category-language such as 'ability to see' or 'as a faculty' or on clinician-only labels such as 'humeral head' and 'distal end' unless Kai's POV directly motivates them.

[CH05_CRAFT_VIRUS_REVEAL_OPACITY] By the end of the revised snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:153-179, a cold reader can state 1) which data source Yusuf consulted beyond Kai's local telemetry, 2) what matching feature across athletes makes him suspect replication, 3) what part is confirmed versus still diagnostic hypothesis, and 4) why grounding the facility follows now.

[CH05_CRAFT_ENDING_OVERSTATEMENT] In the revised ending at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:181-189, the final paragraph must do one new piece of dramatic work beyond restating that Kai's legs ran without him: either a fresh observable change in Kai's relation to his body or an immediate choice or consequence triggered by Yusuf's diagnosis. If the last two sentences can be removed without losing any new dramatic information, the revision still fails.
[R021] Ch 5 contains no more than 7 prose lines with em-dashes (≤~8% of 93 prose lines).
[R044] Ch 5 contains no more than 5 instances of 'something.'

findings.acceptance_test: In the revised snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:25-35 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:51-55, a cold reader can still identify that sight, hearing, and proprioception vanish and that Kai dislocates a shoulder and breaks a wrist, but the passage does not depend on category-language such as 'ability to see' or 'as a faculty' or on clinician-only labels such as 'humeral head' and 'distal end' unless Kai's POV directly motivates them.
findings.problem: The chapter-defining blackout and crash sequence leaves Kai's body-grounded close third for essayistic and clinical language. Phrases like 'the ability to see,' 'as a faculty,' 'humeral head,' and 'distal end' make the narration sound more like an essayist or medic than Kai, so the prose becomes smarter than the focalizer at the exact moment it should feel most inhabited.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:25-35 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:51-55 so the blackout and injuries stay legible through Kai's live bodily language: what disappears, what hits, what slides wrong, what he can and cannot command. Keep the terror physical, but strip category-level abstraction and clinician-only anatomy unless the line is unmistakably something Kai would think in the moment.

findings.acceptance_test: By the end of the revised snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:153-179, a cold reader can state 1) which data source Yusuf consulted beyond Kai's local telemetry, 2) what matching feature across athletes makes him suspect replication, 3) what part is confirmed versus still diagnostic hypothesis, and 4) why grounding the facility follows now.
findings.problem: The chapter asks the reader to accept a plot-critical jump from Kai's crash log to facility-wide infection and immediate quarantine without showing the proof chain. We are told Yusuf sees replication, but the page does not make clear what data source he checked beyond Kai's local log, what shared signature distinguishes a virus from a generic firmware bug, or what part of the diagnosis is still hypothesis.
findings.rewrite_direction: In snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:153-169, show one concrete comparison Yusuf actually pulls up - network handshake logs, a duplicated error hash, matched corruption timestamps, or another specific shared trace - and make his certainty tier explicit before snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:175-179 lands the quarantine decision. Keep the pace, but let the reader see why this is replication rather than authorial fiat.

findings.acceptance_test: In the revised ending at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:181-189, the final paragraph must do one new piece of dramatic work beyond restating that Kai's legs ran without him: either a fresh observable change in Kai's relation to his body or an immediate choice or consequence triggered by Yusuf's diagnosis. If the last two sentences can be removed without losing any new dramatic information, the revision still fails.
findings.problem: By the time the chapter reaches the corridor aftermath, the core horror is already legible: Kai's legs can execute without him. The closing beat keeps paraphrasing that same thesis ('the legs had not needed him,' 'they had done exactly what they wanted') instead of creating a fresh final turn, so the ending certifies meaning the reader already has rather than adding new consequence, decision, or instability.
findings.rewrite_direction: Compress or repurpose snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:181-189. Either end nearer the first ordinary-versus-blackout contrast at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:181-183, or make the foot-flex test in snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:189 produce a genuinely new state shift - hesitation, mistrust, failed obedience, or an immediate action tied to Yusuf's quarantine call - instead of another paraphrase of the chapter's thesis.

findings.acceptance_test: Ch 5 contains no more than 7 prose lines with em-dashes (≤~8% of 93 prose lines).
findings.evidence: Ch 5: 41/93 prose lines contain em-dashes = 44.1%. Manuscript-wide: 654/1876 = 34.9% (threshold 10%, target <8%).
findings.problem: Ch 5 has em-dash density of 44.1%, far exceeding the 10% threshold. The em-dash is used for parenthetical asides, appositives, and mid-sentence pivots at a rate that flattens the prose rhythm. Manuscript target: reduce from 654 to <150 em-dash lines.
findings.rewrite_direction: Reduce em-dash-containing lines in Ch 5 from 41 to ~7 (34 cuts). Convert parenthetical em-dash asides to commas, semicolons, colons, periods, or restructured sentences. Reserve em-dashes for their highest-impact uses: dramatic interruptions, voice-specific rhythms, and moments where the break creates meaning.

findings.acceptance_test: Ch 5 contains no more than 5 instances of 'something.'
findings.evidence: Ch 5: 'something' appears 18 times in 4037 words = 1 per 224 words. Manuscript-wide: 190 occurrences in 78,027 words = 1 per 411 words (threshold: 1 per 800).
findings.problem: 'Something' appears 18 times in Ch 5, a rate of 1 per 224 words. This vague pronoun substitutes for specific imagery. Manuscript-wide target: reduce from 190 to ~98 occurrences.
findings.rewrite_direction: Reduce 'something' in Ch 5 from 18 to ~5 (13 cuts). Replace each cut with the specific noun, sensation, or concept the 'something' stands in for. Keep 'something' only where vagueness is the point (character genuinely cannot name the experience).

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Prioritize structural and craft blockers first: causality, chapter architecture, stakes clarity, continuity logic, and all HIGH/CRITICAL defects.
revision_pass.label: HIGH/CRITICAL structural/craft fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This novel asks what it means to own a body that has been partially replaced, optimized, or networked. The answer cannot be abstract. The physical horror of enhancement failure — sensory blackouts, autonomous muscle firing, personality thinning — must be rendered with visceral specificity. The intimacy between enhanced people must confront the fact that their bodies are partly engineered, and the strangeness of that must be present on the page, not elided. Margot's addiction to the sensory feed must be shown as a genuine addiction with genuine physical craving, not as an interesting character trait. Tomas's paralysis must be ugly, specific, and permanent — not a tidy plot device. The corporate betrayal must feel institutional, not melodramatic. When the novel asks its readers to sit with discomfort — a body that moves without consent, a mind that thins to nothing, an addiction that destroys someone else — the prose must stay present and not retreat to safety. The specific is always braver than the general.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism at the point of consequence is a craft failure. When a body crashes into a wall at sprint speed, the reader should feel the impact — bone, skin, blood, the specific geometry of injury. When Margot feels Tomas's spinal impact through the feed, the sensation must be rendered in full: the compression, the silence of nerves going dead, the phantom that stays after she disconnects. When Kai's body moves without his consent during sex, the reader must experience the uncanny specificity of reflexes that are not his. When Lena loses time, the gap must feel real — not a graceful ellipsis but a genuine absence. Every scene where the novel touches difficult material — bodily horror, addiction, institutional betrayal, the vulnerability of intimacy — must be rendered with the same specificity given to any other moment. Retreat into summary, metaphor, or tasteful vagueness at these moments destroys the novel's central claim that bodies matter.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm for all characters in spoken English. Uncontracted forms are reserved for emphasis, deliberate formality, or character-specific moments (Lena's slightly formal second-language register, Petra's clinical precision). Any uncontracted line in casual dialogue should be checked: does this character have a reason to be formal here?

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate high-intensity chapters with reflective or quiet ones. Vary scene count, dialogue density, and narrative distance across consecutive chapters. A chapter driven by confrontation should not be followed by another confrontation. A chapter heavy in dialogue should be followed by one that relies more on interiority and physical description. The quiet chapters (7, 16, parts of 18) should not feel like connective tissue between the 'real' scenes — they carry the human baseline that makes crisis meaningful. Some chapters should be physically compressed (a single long scene) and others structurally looser (multiple shorter scenes with white space between them).
prose_style_profile.diction: Contemporary literary — technically precise when inhabiting engineering or medical perspectives, grounded in body experience when inhabiting athletic or intimate perspectives. The enhancement technology should be described through felt sensation, not through exposition. How a neural link feels, not how it works. The novel's primary sensory vocabulary is the body: muscle, bone, nerve, heat, pressure, the experience of a body doing things its owner did not command.
forbidden_drift_patterns: Purple proprioception — the sensory descriptions of enhancement should remain specific and physical, not drift into poetic abstraction about the nature of consciousness
Exposition dumps about how the technology works — establish through character experience, not through narrator explanation
Uniform crisis register — not every chapter should feel urgent; some should feel ordinary, empty, or quiet, and the prose should match
Philosophical narrator — the narration should not editorialize about the body-autonomy theme; the events carry the argument
Symmetrical emotional beats — not every character should have matching epiphanies or matching moments of clarity; some characters end muddled
Mechanical metaphors for human experience — the point is that these people are not machines, even when their bodies partly are; do not let the prose treat them as systems
Identical blackout descriptions — each sensory disruption scene must find different language for the experience rather than reusing void/static/white metaphors
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, rotating POV by chapter. The narration inhabits each POV character's sensory and cognitive world — what they notice, how they process sensation, what language they would use internally. The narration should not be smarter, more articulate, or more philosophically aware than the character whose head we are in. Ensemble chapters may shift between characters within scenes but must establish clear transitions.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Variable across chapters and within chapters. Technical/calibration scenes: precise, measured, slightly clinical. Sensory/feed scenes: fractured, overlapping, synaesthetic. Interpersonal scenes: looser, more conversational, allowing silence and dead space. Action/blackout scenes: compressed, fragmented, physical. Quiet scenes: unhurried, sensory, present tense of ordinary experience. No single rhythm should dominate the novel.
